title: Read what Bruce Springsteen had to say about Trump, ICE, and America in Boston Sunday
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen criticized President Trump and his administration during a concert at TD Garden in Boston.
Springsteen highlighted issues with federal troops in Minneapolis, the Voting Rights Act, and immigrant deportations.
He urged the audience to fight for America's ideals and unity, invoking the spirit of civil rights leader John Lewis.

title: Review and setlist: Springsteen eviscerates Trump at fiery TD Garden stop
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bruce Springsteen's TD Garden concert featured fierce criticism of President Trump and was a call to defend American values.
The show included 27 songs, mostly from Springsteen's catalog, with Tom Morello as a special guest guitarist.
Notable performances included 'Streets of Minneapolis' and an urgent version of 'Born in the USA' addressing current political issues.

title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops on May 22, marking his second collaboration with the orchestra.
The concert featured a mix of Broadway, jazz, and American standards, including songs from 'Hamilton' and classics like 'Fly Me To The Moon.'
Odom Jr. expressed appreciation for Symphony Hall, highlighting its history while delivering a powerful performance concluded with audience applause.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, is charged with multiple counts of assault and battery involving children under 14.
He allegedly burned one child with a cigarette lighter and another with a heated fork, according to prosecutors.
Bautista-Zepeda was located in Virginia after fleeing Massachusetts and was arrested; his hearing is set for May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, was convicted of raping a 20-year-old woman in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
Jammal received a 13 ½ to 15-year prison sentence for rape, followed by 5 years of probation for assault.
The victim was assaulted in a hallway and bathroom of the restaurant, and Jammal fled before police arrived.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word, built in 1893, will be converted into condos after city approval.
Habitech Communities purchased the church property for $4 million, planning a six-unit conversion and a new 26-unit building.
The redevelopment reflects a trend in Greater Boston where former churches are repurposed as housing due to shrinking congregations.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade target for the Celtics, offering versatility and defensive skills.
LeBron James is a top unrestricted free agent who could fit the Celtics' roster despite being a long shot.
John Collins could add forward depth with his proven offensive capabilities and strong shooting percentages.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time after defeating Molloy University in a tight series.
Endicott College secured its fourth consecutive trip to the NCAA Division III championship after defeating Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott await details on their upcoming NCAA World Series championship opponents and schedules.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics plan to work out UConn's Alex Karaban, a Southborough native, ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Alex Karaban is ranked No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 draft prospects; Celtics hold picks No. 27 and No. 40.
Karaban leaves UConn as the program's winningest player, with 1,880 points, 126 victories, and 292 three-pointers made.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth's 'Whatever’s Clever! World Tour' at MGM Music Hall emphasized musicianship over spectacle, blending jazz and pop.
The concert featured hits from his new album and older songs, delighting a crowd with musical talents and personal stories.
Openers Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey impressed with performances highlighting their musical abilities, setting the tone for Puth's show.
